revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=北舘神社| 所在地=山形県東田川郡庄内町狩川字笠山400| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|47|11.93|N|139|58|31.66|E|scale:25000}}| 祭神=北舘大学助利長公| 社格=郷社・別表神社| 創建=安永7年（1778年）| 別名=大学様| 例祭=5月1日}}
'''北舘神社'''（きただてじんじゃ）は、[[山形県]][[東田川郡]][[庄内町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[山形藩]][[最上家]]家臣の[[北楯利長|北楯大学助利長]]を祭神とし、'''大学様'''と通称される。
== 概要 == 北舘大学助利長は[[慶長]]6年（[[1601年]]）に[[金山城 (出羽国)|楯山城]]主となり、[[庄内平野]]東部一帯の灌漑を計画、慶長17年（[[1616年]]）に灌漑水路を完成させた。この用水路は「北舘大堰」と呼ばれ、現在でも庄内平野東部の重要な水路となっている。利長が歿した[[寛永]]2年（[[1625年]]）の153年後の[[安永]]7年（[[1778年]]）、この水路の恩恵に与る住民たちが、利長の功績を賛えて狩川八幡宮の境内に利長を祀る祠を設け、「北舘水神社」と称した。[[大正]]8年（[[1919年]]）、社殿を楯山城跡に遷して独立の神社となり、「北舘神社」に改称した。大正11年（[[1922年]]）に[[郷社]]に列し、[[昭和]]36年（[[1961年]]）に[[神社本庁]][[別表神社]]となった。昭和48年（[[1973年]]）現在地の笠山に新社殿を造営し遷座した。
昭和36年竣工の[[海上自衛隊]]の[[護衛艦]]「[[いすず型護衛艦|もがみ]]」には、当社の分霊が守護神として祀られていた。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.k5.dion.ne.jp/~kitadate/ 北舘神社]（公式サイト）

revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=北舘神社 | 画像=[[File:YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kitadatejinja-01.jpg|250px|北舘神社]]
北舘神社拝殿 | 所在地=[[山形県]][[東田川郡]][[庄内町]]狩川字笠山400 | 位置={{Coord|38|47|11.93|N|139|58|31.66|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=北舘神社}} | 祭神=北舘大学助利長公 | 社格=郷社・別表神社 | 創建=[[安永]]7年（[[1778年]]） | 別名=大学様 | 例祭=5月1日 | 地図={{Location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=47|lat_sec=11.93|lon_deg=139|lon_min=58|lon_sec=31.66|label=北舘神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''北舘神社'''（きただてじんじゃ）は、[[山形県]][[東田川郡]][[庄内町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[山形藩]][[最上家]]家臣の[[北楯利長|北楯大学助利長]]を祭神とし、'''大学様'''と通称される。 == 概要 == 北舘大学助利長は[[慶長]]6年（[[1601年]]）に[[金山城 (出羽国)|楯山城]]主となり、[[庄内平野]]東部一帯の灌漑を計画、慶長17年（[[1616年]]）に灌漑水路を完成させた。この用水路は「北舘大堰」と呼ばれ、現在でも庄内平野東部の重要な水路となっている。利長が歿した[[寛永]]2年（[[1625年]]）の153年後の[[安永]]7年（[[1778年]]）、この水路の恩恵に与る住民たちが、利長の功績を賛えて狩川八幡宮の境内に利長を祀る祠を設け、「北舘水神社」と称した。[[大正]]8年（[[1919年]]）、社殿を楯山城跡に遷して独立の神社となり、「北舘神社」に改称した。大正11年（[[1922年]]）に[[郷社]]に列し、[[昭和]]36年（[[1961年]]）に[[神社本庁]][[別表神社]]となった。昭和48年（[[1973年]]）現在地の笠山に新社殿を造営し遷座した。 昭和36年竣工の[[海上自衛隊]]の[[護衛艦]]「[[いすず型護衛艦|もがみ]]」には、当社の分霊が守護神として祀られていた。 == 外部リンク == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.k5.dion.ne.jp/~kitadate/ |title=北舘神社 |date=20060113134436}}（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きたたてしんしや}} [[Category:山形県の神社]] [[Category:山形県の旧郷社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:庄内町の歴史]] [[Category:庄内町の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kitadate Shrine | image=[[File:YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kitadatejinja-01.jpg|250px|Kitadate Shrine]]
Kitadate Shrine Worship Hall | location=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Shonai Town]], Kariyawa, Kasayama 400 | coordinates={{Coord|38|47|11.93|N|139|58|31.66|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitadate Shrine}} | deity=Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga | type=Former Gōsha, Separate Table Shrine | established=[[An'ei]] 7 (1778) | alternative name=Daigakusama | festival=May 1 | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan }} '''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[Mogami clan]] of the [[Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''. == Overview == Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[Tateyama Castle]] in [[Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[Taishō]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. The [[Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.k5.dion.ne.jp/~kitadate/ |title=Kitadate Shrine |date=20060113134436}} (Official Site) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadate Shrine}} [[Category:Separate Table Shrines]] [[Category:History of Shonai Town]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shonai Town]] {{Draft Categories|[[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadate Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[en:Draft:Hokutan Shrine]] }} [[ja:北舘神社]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[Taishō]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. The [[Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.k5.dion.ne.jp/~kitadate/ |title=Kitadate Shrine |date=20060113134436}} (Official Site) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadate Shrine}} [[Category:Separate Table Shrines]] [[Category:History of Shonai Town]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shonai Town]] {{Draft Categories|[[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadate Shrine}}[[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[en:Draft:Hokutan Shrine]] }} [[Category:bump]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[ja:北舘神社]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

Kitadate Shrine Worship Hall | location=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Shonai Town]], Kariyawa, Kasayama 400 | coordinates={{Coord|38|47|11.93|N|139|58|31.66|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitadate Shrine}} | deity=Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga | type=Former Gōsha, Separate Table Shrine | established=[[An'ei]] 7 (1778) | alternative name=Daigakusama | festival=May 1 | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan }} '''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[Mogami clan]] of the [[Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''. == Overview == Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[Tateyama Castle]] in [[Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[Taishō]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. The [[Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.k5.dion.ne.jp/~kitadate/ |title=Kitadate Shrine |date=20060113134436}} (Official Site) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadate Shrine}} [[Category:Separate Table Shrines]] [[Category:History of Shonai Town]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shonai Town]] {{Draft Categories|[[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadate Shrine}}[[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kitadate Shrine]][[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[en:Draft:Hokutan Shrine]] }} [[Category:bump]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:北舘神社]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

Kitadate Shrine Worship Hall | location=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Shonai Town]], Kariyawa, Kasayama 400 | coordinates={{Coord|38|47|11.93|N|139|58|31.66|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitadate Shrine}} | deity=Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga | type=Former Gōsha, Separate Table Shrine | established=[[An'ei]] 7 (1778) | alternative name=Daigakusama | festival=May 1 | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan }} '''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[Mogami clan]] of the [[Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''. == Overview == Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[Tateyama Castle]] in [[Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[Taishō]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. The [[Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.k5.dion.ne.jp/~kitadate/ |title=Kitadate Shrine |date=20060113134436}} (Official Site) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadate Shrine}} [[Category:Separate Table Shrines]] [[Category:History of Shonai Town]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shonai Town]] {{Draft Categories|[[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadate Shrine}}[[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kitadate Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kitadate Shrine]][[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[en:Draft:Hokutan Shrine]] }} [[Category:bump]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kitadate Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kitadate Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Kitadate Shrine]] [[Category:Everybodywiki manual marking]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[ja:北舘神社]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Beppyo Shrines}}

Kitadate Shrine Worship Hall | location=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Shonai Town]], Kariyawa, Kasayama 400 | coordinates={{Coord|38|47|11.93|N|139|58|31.66|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitadate Shrine}} | deity=Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga | type=Former Gōsha, Separate Table Shrine | established=[[An'ei]] 7 (1778) | alternative name=Daigakusama | festival=May 1 | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan }} '''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[Mogami clan]] of the [[Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''. == Overview == Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[Tateyama Castle]] in [[Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[Taishō]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. The [[Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.k5.dion.ne.jp/~kitadate/ |title=Kitadate Shrine |date=20060113134436}} (Official Site) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadate Shrine}} [[Category:Separate Table Shrines]] [[Category:History of Shonai Town]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shonai Town]] {{Draft Categories|[[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitadate Shrine}}[[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kitadate Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kitadate Shrine]][[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[en:Kitadate Shrine]][[en:Draft:Hokutan Shrine]] }} [[Category:bump]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kitadate Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kitadate Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Kitadate Shrine]] [[Category:Everybodywiki manual marking]] [[sn:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kitadate Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:北舘神社]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:ppp]]

Kitadate Shrine Worship Hall | location=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Shonai Town]], Kariyawa, Kasayama 400 | coordinates={{Coord|38|47|11.93|N|139|58|31.66|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitadate Shrine}} | deity=Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga | type=Former Gōsha, Separate Table Shrine | established=[[An'ei]] 7 (1778) | alternative name=Daigakusama | festival=May 1 | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan }} '''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[Mogami clan]] of the [[Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''. == Overview == Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[Tateyama Castle]] in [[Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[Taishō]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. The [[Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity. == External Links == * Kitadate Shrine Worship Hall | location=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Shonai Town]], Kariyawa, Kasayama 400 | coordinates={{Coord|38|47|11.93|N|139|58|31.66|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitadate Shrine}} | deity=Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga | type=Former Gōsha, Separate Table Shrine | established=[[An'ei]] 7 (1778) | alternative name=Daigakusama | festival=May 1 | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan }} '''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[Mogami clan]] of the [[Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''. == Overview == Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[Tateyama Castle]] in [[Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[Taishō]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. The [[Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity. == External Links == * In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[Mogami clan]] of the [[Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''. == Overview == Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[Tateyama Castle]] in [[Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[Taishō]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. The [[Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity. == External Links == * Kitadate Shrine Worship Hall | location=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Shonai Town]], Kariyawa, Kasayama 400 | coordinates={{Coord|38|47|11.93|N|139|58|31.66|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitadate Shrine}} | deity=Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga | type=Former Gōsha, Separate Table Shrine | established=[[An'ei]] 7 (1778) | alternative name=Daigakusama | festival=May 1 | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan }} '''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[Mogami clan]] of the [[Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''. == Overview == Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[Tateyama Castle]] in [[Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[Taishō]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. The [[Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity. == External Links == * Kitadate Shrine Worship Hall | location=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Shonai Town]], Kariyawa, Kasayama 400 | coordinates={{Coord|38|47|11.93|N|139|58|31.66|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitadate Shrine}} | deity=Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga | type=Former Gōsha, Separate Table Shrine | established=[[An'ei]] 7 (1778) | alternative name=Daigakusama | festival=May 1 | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan }} '''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[Mogami clan]] of the [[Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''. == Overview == Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[Tateyama Castle]] in [[Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[Taishō]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. The [[Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity. == External Links == * It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[Mogami clan]] of the [[Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''. == Overview == Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[Tateyama Castle]] in [[Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[Taishō]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. The [[Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity. == External Links == * In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[Mogami clan]] of the [[Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''. == Overview == Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[Tateyama Castle]] in [[Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. In [[Taishō]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. Kitadate Shrine Worship Hall | location=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Shonai Town]], Kariyawa, Kasayama 400 | coordinates={{Coord|38|47|11.93|N|139|58|31.66|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitadate Shrine}} | deity=Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga | type=Former Gōsha, Separate Table Shrine | established=[[An'ei]] 7 (1778) | alternative name=Daigakusama | festival=May 1 | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan }} '''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[Mogami clan]] of the [[Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''. == Overview == Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[Tateyama Castle]] in [[Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[Taishō]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. The [[Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity. == External Links == * It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=[[every:Hokutan Shrine]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:46, 13 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kitadate Shrine | image=[[File:YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kitadatejinja-01.jpg|250px|Kitadate Shrine]]
Kitadate Shrine Worship Hall | location=[[Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Shonai Town]], Kariyawa, Kasayama 400 | coordinates={{Coord|38|47|11.93|N|139|58|31.66|E|scale:25000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitadate Shrine}} | deity=Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga | type=Former Gōsha, Separate Table Shrine | established=[[An'ei]] 7 (1778) | alternative name=Daigakusama | festival=May 1 | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan }} '''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[Mogami clan]] of the [[Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''. == Overview == Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[Tateyama Castle]] in [[Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[Taishō]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama. The [[Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity. == External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.k5.dion.ne.jp/~kitadate/ |title=Kitadate Shrine |date=20060113134436}} (Official Site) {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Kitadate Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
 | name=Kitadate Shrine
 | image=File:YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kitadatejinja-01.jpg
 | location=[[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Shonai Town]], Kariyawa, Kasayama 400
 | coordinates={{Coord|38.786647|N|139.975461|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity=Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga
 | type=Former Gō-sha, Separate Table Shrine
 | established=[[:en:An'ei|An'ei]] 7 (1778)
 | alternative name=Daigakusama
 | festival=May 1
| map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan
| prefecture={{ill|Shonai|qid=Q1348827}}
 }}

'''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] of the [[:en:Yamagata Domain|Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''.

== Overview ==
Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[:en:Tateyama Castle|Tateyama Castle]] in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama.

The [[:en:Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force|Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity.

== External Links ==
* {{Wayback|url=http://www.k5.dion.ne.jp/~kitadate/ |title=Kitadate Shrine |date=20060113134436}} (Official Site)

'''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] of the [[:en:Yamagata Domain|Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''.

== Overview ==
Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[:en:Tateyama Castle|Tateyama Castle]] in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama.

The [[:en:Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force|Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity.

* {{Wayback|url=http://www.k5.dion.ne.jp/~kitadate/ |title=Kitadate Shrine |date=20060113134436}} (Official Site)

'''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] of the [[:en:Yamagata Domain|Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''.

== Overview ==
Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[:en:Tateyama Castle|Tateyama Castle]] in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama.

The [[:en:Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force|Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity.

'''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] of the [[:en:Yamagata Domain|Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''.

== Overview ==
Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[:en:Tateyama Castle|Tateyama Castle]] in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama.

The [[:en:Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force|Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity.

== External Links ==
* {{Wayback|url=http://www.k5.dion.ne.jp/~kitadate/ |title=Kitadate Shrine |date=20060113134436}} (Official Site)

 | established=[[:en:An'ei|An'ei]] 7 (1778)
 | alternative name=Daigakusama
 | festival=May 1
| map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan
| prefecture={{ill|Shonai|WD=Q1348827}}
 }}

'''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the [[:en:Mogami clan|Mogami clan]] of the [[:en:Yamagata Domain|Yamagata Domain]], and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''.

== Overview ==
Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of [[:en:Tateyama Castle|Tateyama Castle]] in [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō era]] 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in [[:en:Shōwa|Shōwa]] 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama.

The [[:en:Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force|Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force]] destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity.

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kitadate Shrine | image=File:YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kitadatejinja-01.jpg | location={{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}}, [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Shonai Town]], Kariyawa, Kasayama 400 | coordinates={{Coord|38.786647|N|139.975461|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga | type=Former Gō-sha, Separate Table Shrine | established={{ill|An'ei|en|An'ei|WD=Q1193910|lt=An'ei}} 7 (1778) | alternative name=Daigakusama | festival=May 1 | map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan | prefecture={{ill|Shonai|WD=Q1348827}} }}
'''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}}. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the {{ill|Mogami clan|en|Mogami clan|WD=Q524625|lt=Mogami clan}} of the {{ill|Yamagata Domain|en|Yamagata Domain|WD=Q908769|lt=Yamagata Domain}}, and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''.
== Overview == Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of {{ill|Tateyama Castle|en|Tateyama Castle|WD=Q2970766|lt=Tateyama Castle}} in {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era}} 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa}} 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama.
The {{ill|Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force|WD=Q731647|lt=Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force}} destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity.
== External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www.k5.dion.ne.jp/~kitadate/ |title=Kitadate Shrine |date=20060113134436}} (Official Site)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
 | name=Kitadate Shrine
 | image=File:YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kitadatejinja-01.jpg
 | location={{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}, [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Shonai Town]], Kariyawa, Kasayama 400
 | coordinates={{Coord|38.786647|N|139.975461|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity=Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga
 | type=Former Gō-sha, Separate Table Shrine
 | established={{ill|An'ei|en|An'ei|WD=Q1193910|lt=An'ei|1=An'ei}} 7 (1778)
 | alternative name=Daigakusama
 | festival=May 1
| map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan
| prefecture={{ill|Shonai|WD=Q1348827|1=Shōnai, Yamagata}}
 }}

'''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the {{ill|Mogami clan|en|Mogami clan|WD=Q524625|lt=Mogami clan|1=Mogami clan}} of the {{ill|Yamagata Domain|en|Yamagata Domain|WD=Q908769|lt=Yamagata Domain|1=Yamagata Domain}}, and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''.

== Overview ==
Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of {{ill|Tateyama Castle|en|Tateyama Castle|WD=Q2970766|lt=Tateyama Castle|1=Tateyama Castle}} in {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama.

The {{ill|Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force|WD=Q731647|lt=Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force|1=Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force}} destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity.

== External Links ==
* {{Wayback|url=http://www.k5.dion.ne.jp/~kitadate/ |title=Kitadate Shrine |date=20060113134436}} (Official Site)

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
 | name=Kitadate Shrine
 | image=File:YamagatakenTuruokashi-Kitadatejinja-01.jpg
 | location={{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|qid=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}, [[Higashitagawa District]], [[Shonai Town]], Kariyawa, Kasayama 400
 | coordinates={{Coord|38.786647|N|139.975461|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity=Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga
 | type=Former Gō-sha, Separate Table Shrine
 | established={{ill|An'ei|en|An'ei|qid=Q1193910|lt=An'ei|1=An'ei}} 7 (1778)
 | alternative name=Daigakusama
 | festival=May 1
| map_type = Japan Yamagata Prefecture#Japan Dewa#Japan
| prefecture={{ill|Shonai|qid=Q1348827|1=Shōnai, Yamagata}}
 }}

'''Kitadate Shrine''' (きただてじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Shonai Town]], [[Higashitagawa District]], {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|qid=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}. It enshrines [[Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga]], a retainer of the {{ill|Mogami clan|en|Mogami clan|qid=Q524625|lt=Mogami clan|1=Mogami clan}} of the {{ill|Yamagata Domain|en|Yamagata Domain|qid=Q908769|lt=Yamagata Domain|1=Yamagata Domain}}, and is commonly referred to as '''Daigakusama'''.

== Overview ==
Kitadate Daigakusuke Toshinaga became the lord of {{ill|Tateyama Castle|en|Tateyama Castle|qid=Q2970766|lt=Tateyama Castle|1=Tateyama Castle}} in {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 6 (1601) and planned the irrigation of the eastern part of the [[Shonai Plain]]. He completed the irrigation canal in Keichō 17 (1616), known as the "Kitadate Great Weir," which remains a crucial waterway for the eastern Shonai Plain. In {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} 2 (1625), 153 years after his death, the local residents benefiting from the waterway erected a shrine dedicated to Toshinaga in the precincts of Kariyawa Hachimangu and called it "Kitadate Suijin Shrine." In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 8 (1919), the shrine was moved to the site of Tateyama Castle and became an independent shrine named "Kitadate Shrine." It was designated a [[Gōsha]] in Taishō 11 (1922) and became a [[Separate Table Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] in {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|qid=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 36 (1961). In Shōwa 48 (1973), a new shrine building was constructed at the current site in Kasayama.

The {{ill|Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force|qid=Q731647|lt=Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force|1=Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force}} destroyer ''[[Mogami-class destroyer|Mogami]]'' commissioned in Shōwa 36 enshrined a spirit of Kitadate Shrine as its guardian deity.
